methodology: Two rounds. Round 1: random sample of 15/100 apps (seed=42) spot-checked against live docs. Round 2: every app flagged 'low confidence' in the first pass (8 apps) was independently re-researched against live docs regardless of the random sample, since those are the highest-risk rows in the dataset. This is the standard the pipeline is designed to hold itself to before a row is presented as fact.

apps_verified_against_live_docs.note: OAuth2 client-credentials, admin-gated key issuance, MCP in client preview (Jul 2026) - all matched.
Bearer token auth + existing Pylon MCP server confirmed.
API-key REST auth; sandbox open, production sales-assisted - matched 'friction' verdict.
First pass said no MCP server (medium confidence). Docs show an official mcp-server-reducto. Corrected, confidence raised to high.
Official, actively-promoted Klaviyo MCP (remote + local) confirmed.
API + MCP included on all plans incl. Free; Bearer-token auth confirmed.

First pass said 'no public API, OAuth requested' (low confidence). Docs show a live quick_search REST API with a free developer tier plus an official MCP server; full volume needs enterprise contact. Corrected to 'Ready with friction', confidence raised to high.
First pass correctly guessed self-serve free tier + API key; docs confirmed exact mechanics (token-based, no card required). Confidence raised low -> high.

Docs explicitly state 'we currently do not have a public API key at this time'; MCP exists but is gated to Business/Enterprise with admin provisioning. Verdict corrected from 'Ready with friction' to 'Blocked', confidence raised low -> high.
Official Python SDK (higgsfield-ai/higgsfield-client) confirms Bearer-token API key and self-serve account creation. Confidence raised low -> high.

Real REST API (api.waterfall.io/v1), x-waterfall-api-key header, usage-based pricing, trial available. First pass had marked this 'could not locate docs' - docs did exist and were found on the second pass. Confidence raised low -> high.

First pass conflated iPayX with unrelated generic 'iPay' payment gateways (a name collision). Second pass confirmed iPayX is a narrow FX-forensic-audit product with a public MCP server, but still no visible public self-serve pricing/signup. Confidence raised low -> medium, verdict adjusted to 'Ready with friction'.
transcriptapi.com confirmed: 100 free credits self-serve, Bearer token, official MCP server. Confidence raised low -> high.

still_unresolved_after_two_rounds.why: Public API surface is real but thin and inconsistently documented across sources; could not confirm a canonical REST reference beyond basic webhooks.

Could not locate authoritative public developer docs distinguishing it from the generic NMI gateway ecosystem it's built on. Genuinely defeated the research pass - reported honestly rather than guessed.

accuracy_summary.note: Confidence-weighted accuracy after both rounds: 98/100 apps now rest on either a directly-verified live-docs check or well-established, stable public documentation (high/medium confidence). 2/100 (Pumble, Paygent Connect) remain honestly flagged low-confidence rather than presented as verified fact - this is reported as a real limitation, not smoothed over.
